generation_contract.positive_template: A {size} {life_stage} {breed} {species} with a {body_build} anatomically normal body, a {coat_color} {coat_length} coat with a {coat_pattern} pattern, {ear_shape} ears, and a {tail_shape} tail.
generation_contract.pose_guard_prompt: Preserve the exact authored neutral rigging stance, silhouette, and joint locations from the input. Use the input's orthographic exact side view with the camera level at torso height. The torso and spine must remain one straight horizontal longitudinal axis with zero yaw toward the camera. The head and neck must align with that axis; the animal must not look toward or away from the camera and must not tilt its head. Preserve all four individually visible legs: only the far-side front and hind limb chains have small deterministic opposite fore-aft offsets, while the torso and near-side limbs remain in the authored rest pose. Do not convert these compact offsets into a walking stride or show-dog stack. Keep all four paws planted on the same level ground plane. Keep the tail separated from the body, preserve full-body framing, and use a plain background. This is a static rigging reference, not an action pose.
generation_contract.negative_prompt: three-quarter view, perspective view, camera yaw, torso yaw, twisted torso, twisted spine, curved body axis, head turned toward camera, head turned away from camera, tilted head, walking, running, gait stride, lifted paw, crouching, sitting, lying down, uneven ground, mismatched paw height, crossed legs, splayed legs, cropped body, sitting, lying down, merged legs, hidden paws, fused tail, extra limbs, colorpoint pattern, background geometry, text

reference_provenance.notes: Provisional pipeline target only; replace with a license-snapshotted species reference and measured canary before formal registration.

coat_color.label: coat color
coat_color.identification_question: What is the coat color of {instance_label}?

body_build.label: body build
body_build.comparison_question: Which tabby cat has the stockier build?
